title: The unopened letter
commandDescriptor: Work out what is suggested by clues, even when it is not directly stated.
skillFocus: Using clues to make a sensible inference
answerPattern: Answer stem → inference → clue → explanation of clue
teachingTip: Do not guess wildly. Use a clue and explain what it suggests.
text: A white envelope lay on the kitchen table. Mum walked past it twice without touching it. When Dad asked if she had opened it, she shook her head and began wiping an already clean worktop.
textHtml: A white envelope lay on the kitchen table. Mum walked past it twice without touching it. When Dad asked if she had opened it, she shook her head and began wiping an already clean worktop.
question: What can you infer about Mum's feelings towards the letter?
weakExplanation: This only states what is directly in the text; it does not infer her feelings.
whatQuestionTheWeakAnswerWouldAnswer: What is on the kitchen table?
exceptionalAnswer: I can infer that Mum feels nervous about the letter. She walks past it twice without touching it and wipes an already clean worktop, which suggests she is avoiding the letter and trying to distract herself.

answerParts.label: Answer stem
answerParts.text: I can infer that

answerParts.label: Inference
answerParts.text: Mum feels nervous about the letter.

answerParts.label: Clue
answerParts.text: She walks past it twice without touching it and wipes an already clean worktop,

answerParts.label: Explanation
answerParts.text: which suggests she is avoiding the letter and trying to distract herself.

breakdown.label: Answer stem
breakdown.text: I can infer that
breakdown.note: This turns the question into the beginning of a clear full-sentence answer.

breakdown.label: Inference
breakdown.text: Mum feels nervous about the letter.
breakdown.note: This states what can be worked out from clues.

breakdown.label: Clue
breakdown.text: She walks past it twice without touching it and wipes an already clean worktop,
breakdown.note: This gives the clue from the text that supports the inference.

breakdown.label: Explanation
breakdown.text: which suggests she is avoiding the letter and trying to distract herself.
breakdown.note: This explains how the evidence proves the point.

title: The muddy trainers
commandDescriptor: Work out what is suggested by clues, even when it is not directly stated.
skillFocus: Using clues to make a sensible inference
answerPattern: Answer stem → inference → clue → explanation of clue
teachingTip: Do not guess wildly. Use a clue and explain what it suggests.
text: Noah crept through the back door carrying his trainers in one hand. Mud dripped from the laces onto the tiles. "Were you playing near the pond?" asked Grandma. Noah looked down at his socks.
textHtml: Noah crept through the back door carrying his trainers in one hand. Mud dripped from the laces onto the tiles. "Were you playing near the pond?" asked Grandma. Noah looked down at his socks.
question: What can you infer about where Noah has been?
weakExplanation: This gives a clue, but it does not say what you can work out from it.
exceptionalAnswer: I can infer that Noah has probably been playing near the pond or somewhere very muddy. His trainers are dripping mud, and Grandma asks if he was near the pond, which suggests the mud gives away where he has been.

answerParts.label: Answer stem
answerParts.text: I can infer that

answerParts.label: Inference
answerParts.text: Noah has probably been playing near the pond or somewhere very muddy.

answerParts.label: Clue
answerParts.text: His trainers are dripping mud, and Grandma asks if he was near the pond,

answerParts.label: Explanation
answerParts.text: which suggests the mud gives away where he has been.

breakdown.label: Answer stem
breakdown.text: I can infer that
breakdown.note: This turns the question into the beginning of a clear full-sentence answer.

breakdown.label: Inference
breakdown.text: Noah has probably been playing near the pond or somewhere very muddy.
breakdown.note: This states what can be worked out from clues.

breakdown.label: Clue
breakdown.text: His trainers are dripping mud, and Grandma asks if he was near the pond,
breakdown.note: This gives the clue from the text that supports the inference.

breakdown.label: Explanation
breakdown.text: which suggests the mud gives away where he has been.
breakdown.note: This explains how the evidence proves the point.

title: The spelling test
commandDescriptor: Work out what is suggested by clues, even when it is not directly stated.
skillFocus: Using clues to make a sensible inference
answerPattern: Answer stem → inference → clue → explanation of clue
teachingTip: Do not guess wildly. Use a clue and explain what it suggests.
text: Amelia folded the test paper and slid it under her book. When her friend asked her score, Amelia shrugged and said, "It doesn't matter." Her eyes stayed fixed on the desk.
textHtml: Amelia folded the test paper and slid it under her book. When her friend asked her score, Amelia shrugged and said, "It doesn't matter." Her eyes stayed fixed on the desk.
question: What can you infer about Amelia's test result?
weakExplanation: This repeats what Amelia says, but it does not infer what her behaviour suggests.
exceptionalAnswer: I can infer that Amelia is disappointed with her test result. She hides the paper, avoids looking at her friend and says it does not matter, which suggests she may be embarrassed or upset about her score.

answerParts.label: Answer stem
answerParts.text: I can infer that

answerParts.label: Inference
answerParts.text: Amelia is disappointed with her test result.

answerParts.label: Clue
answerParts.text: She hides the paper, avoids looking at her friend and says it does not matter,

answerParts.label: Explanation
answerParts.text: which suggests she may be embarrassed or upset about her score.

breakdown.label: Answer stem
breakdown.text: I can infer that
breakdown.note: This turns the question into the beginning of a clear full-sentence answer.

breakdown.label: Inference
breakdown.text: Amelia is disappointed with her test result.
breakdown.note: This states what can be worked out from clues.

breakdown.label: Clue
breakdown.text: She hides the paper, avoids looking at her friend and says it does not matter,
breakdown.note: This gives the clue from the text that supports the inference.

breakdown.label: Explanation
breakdown.text: which suggests she may be embarrassed or upset about her score.
breakdown.note: This explains how the evidence proves the point.

title: The empty stage
commandDescriptor: Work out what is suggested by clues, even when it is not directly stated.
skillFocus: Using clues to make a sensible inference
answerPattern: Answer stem → inference → clue → explanation of clue
teachingTip: Do not guess wildly. Use a clue and explain what it suggests.
text: The curtains were still closed, but Malik could hear chairs scraping and programmes rustling beyond them. He rubbed his hands on his trousers and took three slow breaths.
textHtml: The curtains were still closed, but Malik could hear chairs scraping and programmes rustling beyond them. He rubbed his hands on his trousers and took three slow breaths.
question: What can you infer about Malik?
weakExplanation: This says where Malik is, but it does not work out how he feels.
exceptionalAnswer: I can infer that Malik is about to perform and feels nervous. He hears the audience beyond the curtains, rubs his hands on his trousers and takes slow breaths, which suggests he is trying to calm himself.

answerParts.label: Explanation
answerParts.text: which suggests he is trying to calm himself.

breakdown.note: This turns the question into the beginning of a clear full-sentence answer.

breakdown.label: Inference
breakdown.text: Malik is about to perform and feels nervous.
breakdown.note: This states what can be worked out from clues.

breakdown.label: Clue
breakdown.text: He hears the audience beyond the curtains, rubs his hands on his trousers and takes slow breaths,
breakdown.note: This gives the clue from the text that supports the inference.

breakdown.label: Explanation
breakdown.text: which suggests he is trying to calm himself.
breakdown.note: This explains how the evidence proves the point.

title: The broken vase
commandDescriptor: Work out what is suggested by clues, even when it is not directly stated.
skillFocus: Using clues to make a sensible inference
answerPattern: Answer stem → inference → clue → explanation of clue
teachingTip: Do not guess wildly. Use a clue and explain what it suggests.
text: The vase lay in pieces beside the sofa. The cat slept peacefully upstairs, but a foam football rested under the coffee table. Daniel stood with his hands behind his back.
textHtml: The vase lay in pieces beside the sofa. The cat slept peacefully upstairs, but a foam football rested under the coffee table. Daniel stood with his hands behind his back.
question: What can you infer about how the vase broke?
weakExplanation: This notices the result, but it does not infer the likely cause.
exceptionalAnswer: I can infer that the vase was probably broken by the football, not by the cat. The cat is asleep upstairs, but the foam football is under the coffee table and Daniel is hiding his hands, which suggests he may have kicked or thrown it indoors.

answerParts.label: Answer stem
answerParts.text: I can infer that

answerParts.label: Inference
answerParts.text: the vase was probably broken by the football, not by the cat.

answerParts.label: Clue
answerParts.text: The cat is asleep upstairs, but the foam football is under the coffee table and Daniel is hiding his hands,

answerParts.label: Explanation
answerParts.text: which suggests he may have kicked or thrown it indoors.

breakdown.label: Answer stem
breakdown.text: I can infer that
breakdown.note: This turns the question into the beginning of a clear full-sentence answer.

breakdown.label: Inference
breakdown.text: the vase was probably broken by the football, not by the cat.
breakdown.note: This states what can be worked out from clues.

breakdown.label: Clue
breakdown.text: The cat is asleep upstairs, but the foam football is under the coffee table and Daniel is hiding his hands,
breakdown.note: This gives the clue from the text that supports the inference.

breakdown.label: Explanation
breakdown.text: which suggests he may have kicked or thrown it indoors.
breakdown.note: This explains how the evidence proves the point.

title: The secret recipe
commandDescriptor: Work out what is suggested by clues, even when it is not directly stated.
skillFocus: Using clues to make a sensible inference
answerPattern: Answer stem → inference → clue → explanation of clue
teachingTip: Do not guess wildly. Use a clue and explain what it suggests.
text: Auntie Priya closed the notebook as soon as Sam entered the kitchen. Flour dusted her sleeves, and the smell of cinnamon filled the room. "Nothing to see yet," she said, smiling.
textHtml: Auntie Priya closed the notebook as soon as Sam entered the kitchen. Flour dusted her sleeves, and the smell of cinnamon filled the room. "Nothing to see yet," she said, smiling.
question: What can you infer Auntie Priya is doing?
weakExplanation: This is directly stated, but it does not infer her activity from the clues.
exceptionalAnswer: I can infer that Auntie Priya is baking something as a surprise. Flour is on her sleeves, the room smells of cinnamon and she hides the notebook, which suggests she is using a recipe and does not want Sam to know yet.

answerParts.label: Answer stem
answerParts.text: I can infer that

answerParts.label: Inference
answerParts.text: Auntie Priya is baking something as a surprise.

answerParts.label: Clue
answerParts.text: Flour is on her sleeves, the room smells of cinnamon and she hides the notebook,

answerParts.label: Explanation
answerParts.text: which suggests she is using a recipe and does not want Sam to know yet.

breakdown.label: Answer stem
breakdown.text: I can infer that
breakdown.note: This turns the question into the beginning of a clear full-sentence answer.

breakdown.label: Inference
breakdown.text: Auntie Priya is baking something as a surprise.
breakdown.note: This states what can be worked out from clues.

breakdown.label: Clue
breakdown.text: Flour is on her sleeves, the room smells of cinnamon and she hides the notebook,
breakdown.note: This gives the clue from the text that supports the inference.

breakdown.label: Explanation
breakdown.text: which suggests she is using a recipe and does not want Sam to know yet.
breakdown.note: This explains how the evidence proves the point.

title: The late bus
commandDescriptor: Work out what is suggested by clues, even when it is not directly stated.
skillFocus: Using clues to make a sensible inference
answerPattern: Answer stem → inference → clue → explanation of clue
teachingTip: Do not guess wildly. Use a clue and explain what it suggests.
text: Ellie stood at the bus stop long after the other pupils had left. She kept checking the road, then the timetable, then her phone. Her shoulders dropped when another red car turned the corner instead of the bus.
textHtml: Ellie stood at the bus stop long after the other pupils had left. She kept checking the road, then the timetable, then her phone. Her shoulders dropped when another red car turned the corner instead of the bus.
question: What can you infer about Ellie's journey?
weakAnswer: Ellie is at a bus stop.
weakExplanation: This gives the setting, but it does not infer what is happening with her journey.
exceptionalAnswer: I can infer that Ellie's bus is late and she is becoming disappointed. She keeps checking the road, timetable and phone, and her shoulders drop when the bus does not arrive, which suggests she has been waiting anxiously.

answerParts.label: Answer stem
answerParts.text: I can infer that

answerParts.label: Inference
answerParts.text: Ellie's bus is late and she is becoming disappointed.

answerParts.label: Clue
answerParts.text: She keeps checking the road, timetable and phone, and her shoulders drop when the bus does not arrive,

answerParts.label: Explanation
answerParts.text: which suggests she has been waiting anxiously.

breakdown.label: Answer stem
breakdown.text: I can infer that
breakdown.note: This turns the question into the beginning of a clear full-sentence answer.

breakdown.label: Inference
breakdown.text: Ellie's bus is late and she is becoming disappointed.
breakdown.note: This states what can be worked out from clues.

breakdown.label: Clue
breakdown.text: She keeps checking the road, timetable and phone, and her shoulders drop when the bus does not arrive,
breakdown.note: This gives the clue from the text that supports the inference.

breakdown.label: Explanation
breakdown.text: which suggests she has been waiting anxiously.
breakdown.note: This explains how the evidence proves the point.

title: The new shoes
commandDescriptor: Work out what is suggested by clues, even when it is not directly stated.
skillFocus: Using clues to make a sensible inference
answerPattern: Answer stem → inference → clue → explanation of clue
teachingTip: Do not guess wildly. Use a clue and explain what it suggests.
text: Ravi walked across the playground without running, even when his friends called him to join the game. He looked down every few steps at his bright white trainers and stepped around the muddy puddles.
textHtml: Ravi walked across the playground without running, even when his friends called him to join the game. He looked down every few steps at his bright white trainers and stepped around the muddy puddles.
question: What can you infer about Ravi's new trainers?
weakExplanation: This gives a detail, but it does not explain what Ravi's behaviour suggests about them.
exceptionalAnswer: I can infer that Ravi wants to keep his new trainers clean. He keeps looking at them and steps around the muddy puddles, which suggests he is proud of them and does not want them to get dirty.

answerParts.text: I can infer that

answerParts.text: Ravi wants to keep his new trainers clean.

answerParts.label: Explanation
answerParts.text: which suggests he is proud of them and does not want them to get dirty.

breakdown.text: He keeps looking at them and steps around the muddy puddles,
breakdown.note: This gives the clue from the text that supports the inference.

breakdown.text: which suggests he is proud of them and does not want them to get dirty.

title: The quiet birthday
commandDescriptor: Work out what is suggested by clues, even when it is not directly stated.
skillFocus: Using clues to make a sensible inference
answerPattern: Answer stem → inference → clue → explanation of clue
teachingTip: Do not guess wildly. Use a clue and explain what it suggests.
text: There were no balloons in the hallway and no cake on the table. Still, Leila noticed Dad whispering into his phone and her brother carrying a large box upstairs under his jumper.
textHtml: There were no balloons in the hallway and no cake on the table. Still, Leila noticed Dad whispering into his phone and her brother carrying a large box upstairs under his jumper.
question: What can you infer is happening?
weakExplanation: This notices one detail, but it misses the clues that something is being hidden.
whatQuestionTheWeakAnswerWouldAnswer: What is not in the hallway?
exceptionalAnswer: I can infer that Leila's family is secretly preparing a birthday surprise. Although there are no balloons or cake visible, Dad is whispering and her brother hides a large box, which suggests they are keeping the preparations hidden.

answerParts.text: I can infer that

answerParts.text: Leila's family is secretly preparing a birthday surprise.

answerParts.label: Explanation
answerParts.text: which suggests they are keeping the preparations hidden.

breakdown.text: I can infer that
breakdown.note: This turns the question into the beginning of a clear full-sentence answer.

breakdown.text: Leila's family is secretly preparing a birthday surprise.
breakdown.note: This states what can be worked out from clues.

breakdown.label: Clue
breakdown.text: Although there are no balloons or cake visible, Dad is whispering and her brother hides a large box,
breakdown.note: This gives the clue from the text that supports the inference.

breakdown.label: Explanation
breakdown.text: which suggests they are keeping the preparations hidden.
breakdown.note: This explains how the evidence proves the point.

title: The match report
commandDescriptor: Work out what is suggested by clues, even when it is not directly stated.
skillFocus: Using clues to make a sensible inference
answerPattern: Answer stem → inference → clue → explanation of clue
teachingTip: Do not guess wildly. Use a clue and explain what it suggests.
text: The classroom door opened and the football team came in silently. Their shirts were splashed with rain, and the captain placed the silver runners-up medal on the teacher's desk without speaking.
textHtml: The classroom door opened and the football team came in silently. Their shirts were splashed with rain, and the captain placed the silver runners-up medal on the teacher's desk without speaking.
question: What can you infer about the football match?
weakExplanation: This is obvious from the passage, but it does not infer the result or mood.
whatQuestionTheWeakAnswerWouldAnswer: What sport did the team play?
exceptionalAnswer: I can infer that the team lost the final or came second and feels disappointed. The captain places a runners-up medal on the desk without speaking, and the team enters silently, which suggests they are unhappy with the result.

answerParts.text: I can infer that

answerParts.label: Inference
answerParts.text: the team lost the final or came second and feels disappointed.

answerParts.label: Explanation
answerParts.text: which suggests they are unhappy with the result.

breakdown.text: the team lost the final or came second and feels disappointed.

breakdown.text: The captain places a runners-up medal on the desk without speaking, and the team enters silently,
breakdown.note: This gives the clue from the text that supports the inference.
